title: UK Kidney Association Updated commentary on NlCE guideline (NG136) Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management including proposals for blood pressure management in patients with chronic kidney disease, with a brief commentary on KDlGO Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Blood Pressure in Chronic Kidney Disease 2021
summary:
This document provides updated recommendations for managing hypertension in adults, specifically addressing patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD). It recommends using standardized office blood pressure measurements for treatment decisions and suggests a blood pressure target of less than 130/80 mmHg for individuals with CKD, provided it is tolerated after shared-decision making. The document also includes proposals regarding blood pressure targets for patients without CKD, lifestyle modifications like reducing salt intake, and the importance of adhering to these recommendations.

The document also includes proposals regarding blood pressure targets for patients without CKD, lifestyle modifications like reducing salt intake, and the importance of adhering to these recommendations.
